entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A cereal grain crop grown extensively in the Asian-Pacific region

entities.name: ASIAN-PACIFIC REGION
entities.description: The geographical area where rice cultivation and weed management are discussed

entities.name: WEED MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of controlling weeds to increase crop productivity

entities.name: MANUAL WEEDING
entities.description: A weed control method involving the physical removal of weeds by hand

entities.name: LAND PREPARATION
entities.description: A weed control method involving the preparation of the soil before planting

entities.name: CULTURAL METHODS
entities.description: Agricultural practices used to manage weeds, such as manipulating cultivar and planting density

entities.name: PLANTING DENSITY
entities.description: The spacing of plants used as a cultural method for weed control

entities.name: WATER LEVEL MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The control of water levels in rice fields to manage weed growth

entities.name: HERBICIDE APPLICATION
entities.description: The use of chemical substances to kill or inhibit the growth of weeds

entities.name: CROP ROTATIONS
entities.description: The practice of growing different crops in succession on the same land to manage weeds

entities.name: CROP RESIDUES USE
entities.description: The management and use of leftover crop materials to suppress weeds

entities.name: PREVENTATIVE WEED CONTROL
entities.description: Measures taken to prevent the introduction of weeds, such as using clean seeds and equipment

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL CONTROL
entities.description: The use of living organisms to control weed populations

entities.name: HERBICIDE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The inherited ability of weeds to tolerate or survive herbicide treatment

entities.name: RICE WEEDS
entities.description: Unwanted plants that grow in rice fields and compete with the crop

entities.name: WEED FLORAS
entities.description: The composition and distribution of weed species in a given area

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns affecting agriculture

entities.name: RICE PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of rice yield per unit area

entities.name: ALLELOPATHY
entities.description: The chemical inhibition of one plant by another, used in some rice cultivars for weed management

entities.name: DIRECT-SEEDING
entities.description: A method of sowing rice seeds directly into the field

entities.name: TRANSPLANTING
entities.description: A method of planting rice seedlings into the field

entities.name: WEEDY RICE
entities.description: A specific, serious weed problem consisting of rice-like plants

entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Principles of managing natural resources to sustain productivity

entities.name: SOCIO-ECONOMIC FACTORS
entities.description: Social and economic variables that influence farmers' decision-making in weed management
